name: Reply & Forward
theme: Respond to and forward existing messages with the same safety guarantees as compose.

criteria.description: 'mailctl reply <message-id>' command exists as a top-level command, accepts a positional message-id argument, and succeeds (exit 0) when given a valid id and body source with mocked osascript
testSteps: Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body "Thanks"' via CliRunner with mock_osascript configured to return original-message data for the fetch call and a draft id for the reply call
Verify exit code is 0
Verify mock_osascript was invoked (at least one call to fetch the original message, at least one to create the reply)
criteria.threshold: Command succeeds with exit code 0 and at least two mocked osascript calls (fetch + reply)

criteria.description: 'mailctl reply <id>' (without --all) sets the reply recipient to the original message's sender only; no other recipients from the original To/Cc are included
testSteps: Configure mock_osascript to return an original message with sender='alice@example.com', to='user@example.com, bob@example.com', cc='carol@example.com'
Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body "Got it"' via CliRunner
Inspect the reply AppleScript in mock_osascript.calls
Verify the script creates a 'to recipient' for 'alice@example.com' only
Verify the script does NOT include bob@example.com or carol@example.com as recipients
criteria.threshold: Reply-to-sender addresses only the original sender; original To/Cc are excluded

criteria.description: 'mailctl reply <id> --all' sets recipients to: original sender + all original To recipients (minus the user) + all original Cc recipients
testSteps: Configure mock_osascript to return an original message with sender='alice@example.com', to='user@example.com, bob@example.com', cc='carol@example.com'
Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --all --body "Agreed"' via CliRunner
Inspect the reply AppleScript in mock_osascript.calls
Verify 'to recipient' entries exist for alice@example.com and bob@example.com
Verify 'cc recipient' entry exists for carol@example.com
Verify the script does not duplicate the user's own address as a recipient
criteria.threshold: Reply-all includes original sender and all original recipients with correct to/cc classification

criteria.description: 'mailctl forward <message-id> --to <address>' command exists as a top-level command; --to is required and repeatable; missing --to produces a usage error
testSteps: Run 'mailctl forward 12345 --to dave@example.com --body "FYI"' with mock_osascript returning original-message data and a draft id
Verify exit code is 0
Run 'mailctl forward 12345 --body "FYI"' (no --to) and verify exit code is non-zero
Run 'mailctl forward 12345 --to a@x.com --to b@x.com --body "FYI"' and verify both addresses appear in the generated AppleScript
criteria.threshold: Forward command succeeds with --to, fails without it, and supports multiple --to addresses

criteria.description: Reply and forward both include the original message content as a quoted section in the message body, visually separated from the user's new text
testSteps: Configure mock_osascript to return an original message with body='Original body text', sender='alice@example.com', date='Monday, April 14, 2025 at 10:00:00 AM', subject='Hello'
Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body "My reply"'
Inspect the generated AppleScript body content and verify it contains both 'My reply' and 'Original body text'
Verify the original content is preceded by a separator or attribution line (e.g. 'On <date>, <sender> wrote:' or similar)
Run 'mailctl forward 12345 --to d@x.com --body "See below"' and verify the same pattern: user body + original content with attribution
criteria.threshold: Both reply and forward include the user's new body prepended to a quoted/attributed original message

criteria.description: Reply and forward accept body via --body <text>, --body-file <path>, or piped stdin, following the same rules as compose (exactly one source required)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body "inline"' and verify the body appears in the generated AppleScript
Write 'file content' to a temp file; run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body-file <path>' and verify 'file content' appears
Run 'mailctl reply 12345' with stdin piped as 'piped content' and verify it appears
Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body X --body-file Y' and verify it errors (two sources)
Repeat at least one body-source test with 'mailctl forward' to confirm parity
criteria.threshold: All three body sources work for reply and forward; supplying two is a usage error

criteria.description: '--attach <path>' is repeatable on both reply and forward; attachment paths are validated before AppleScript execution
testSteps: Create temp files and run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body B --attach /tmp/a.txt --attach /tmp/b.txt'
Verify generated AppleScript references both attachment paths
Run 'mailctl forward 12345 --to a@x.com --body B --attach /tmp/a.txt' and verify attachment appears
Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body B --attach /nonexistent' and verify non-zero exit with error mentioning the missing file
criteria.threshold: Attachments are passed through to AppleScript; missing files fail fast before any message is created

criteria.description: DEFAULT BEHAVIOUR: 'mailctl reply' WITHOUT '--dangerously-send' creates a draft and the generated AppleScript NEVER contains the 'send' verb
testSteps: Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body "Thanks"' with mock_osascript (no --dangerously-send)
Inspect every script string in mock_osascript.calls
Assert NONE of the script strings contain the AppleScript 'send' verb
Assert a draft-creation script is present (references 'make new outgoing message' or 'reply' construct without 'send')
Verify exit code 0 and output reports a draft was created
criteria.threshold: Zero scripts contain 'send' when --dangerously-send is absent; a draft is created

criteria.description: DEFAULT BEHAVIOUR: 'mailctl forward' WITHOUT '--dangerously-send' creates a draft and the generated AppleScript NEVER contains the 'send' verb
testSteps: Run 'mailctl forward 12345 --to a@x.com --body "FYI"' with mock_osascript (no --dangerously-send)
Inspect every script string in mock_osascript.calls
Assert NONE of the script strings contain the AppleScript 'send' verb
Verify exit code 0 and output reports a draft was created
criteria.threshold: Zero scripts contain 'send' when --dangerously-send is absent; a draft is created

criteria.description: '--dangerously-send --yes' on reply triggers the send code path; verified via mocked AppleScript inspection only (NEVER by real send)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body B --dangerously-send --yes' with mock_osascript
Inspect mock_osascript.calls and verify a script DOES contain the 'send' verb
Verify exit code 0 and output reports the message was sent
IMPORTANT: verified via mock only — never against real Mail.app
criteria.threshold: With --dangerously-send --yes, the generated AppleScript contains 'send'; verified via mock only

criteria.description: '--dangerously-send --yes' on forward triggers the send code path; verified via mocked AppleScript inspection only (NEVER by real send)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl forward 12345 --to a@x.com --body B --dangerously-send --yes' with mock_osascript
Inspect mock_osascript.calls and verify a script DOES contain the 'send' verb
Verify exit code 0 and output reports the message was sent
IMPORTANT: verified via mock only — never against real Mail.app
criteria.threshold: With --dangerously-send --yes, the generated AppleScript contains 'send'; verified via mock only

criteria.description: Confirmation prompt on reply/forward with --dangerously-send (without --yes): declining (default N or empty input) aborts without invoking 'send'
testSteps: Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body B --dangerously-send' with CliRunner input='' (user presses enter — default No)
Verify NO script in mock_osascript.calls contains 'send'
Verify output states send was cancelled
Run 'mailctl forward 12345 --to a@x.com --body B --dangerously-send' with input='n\n'
Verify NO 'send' verb in any script
criteria.threshold: Default-to-No confirmation prevents 'send' verb from reaching AppleScript for both reply and forward

criteria.description: '--yes' alone (without --dangerously-send) on reply/forward NEVER causes a send; it only suppresses the confirmation prompt
testSteps: Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body B --yes' (NO --dangerously-send) with mock_osascript
Verify exit 0, a draft was created, and NO script contains 'send'
Run 'mailctl forward 12345 --to a@x.com --body B --yes' (NO --dangerously-send)
Verify exit 0, a draft was created, and NO script contains 'send'
criteria.threshold: --yes without --dangerously-send creates a draft, never sends

criteria.description: No env var, config file, or alias mechanism can set --dangerously-send implicitly on reply or forward commands; verified by code inspection
testSteps: Inspect the reply and forward command implementations
Verify the --dangerously-send Typer option has NO 'envvar=' parameter
Grep src/ for any env var, config file, or default-flag mechanism that could set dangerously_send for reply or forward
Confirm the safety-as-architecture principle holds: the only way to send is the literal CLI flag
criteria.threshold: No implicit send mechanism exists for reply or forward

criteria.description: '--dry-run' on reply prints what WOULD happen without executing the reply AppleScript; original message fetch IS allowed (read-only)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body B --dry-run' with mock_osascript
Verify exit code 0 and stdout describes a reply would be created as a DRAFT
Verify no script in mock_osascript.calls contains 'make new outgoing message' or 'send' (read-only fetch of original message is acceptable)
Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body B --dangerously-send --yes --dry-run' and verify stdout describes a reply WOULD BE SENT, but no send is actually executed
criteria.threshold: --dry-run produces accurate description and never executes the reply/send AppleScript

criteria.description: '--dry-run' on forward prints what WOULD happen without executing the forward AppleScript
testSteps: Run 'mailctl forward 12345 --to a@x.com --body B --dry-run' with mock_osascript
Verify exit code 0 and stdout describes a forward would be created as a DRAFT
Verify no compose/send AppleScript was executed (read-only original-message fetch is acceptable)
Run 'mailctl forward 12345 --to a@x.com --body B --dangerously-send --yes --dry-run' and verify stdout describes WOULD SEND but no send is executed
criteria.threshold: --dry-run produces accurate description and never executes the forward/send AppleScript

criteria.description: Reply AppleScript preserves message threading by referencing the original message (e.g. setting In-Reply-To, using Mail.app's reply construct, or referencing the original message in the script)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body B' with mock_osascript
Inspect the generated reply AppleScript

Verify the script references the original message id (12345) in a way that supports threading — e.g. by using Mail.app's 'reply' verb on the original message, or by setting reply-to headers, or by constructing the outgoing message with a reference to the original
criteria.threshold: Reply AppleScript includes a reference to the original message to support threading

criteria.description: Reply and forward produce human-readable confirmation output stating whether a draft was created or message was sent, including the subject and account
testSteps: Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body B' (draft path) and capture stdout
Verify stdout contains 'draft' (case-insensitive) and mentions the subject
Run 'mailctl forward 12345 --to a@x.com --body B' and verify similar draft confirmation
Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body B --dangerously-send --yes' and verify stdout mentions 'sent'
criteria.threshold: Human-readable output clearly distinguishes draft creation from sending

criteria.description: '--json' on reply and forward emits a structured JSON object with at least: action ('draft' or 'sent'), to (list), subject, and id; forward also includes the original message id
testSteps: Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body B --json' with mock_osascript
Parse stdout as JSON and verify it is an object with keys: action (== 'draft'), to, subject, and id
Run 'mailctl forward 12345 --to a@x.com --body B --json'
Parse stdout as JSON and verify action == 'draft', to contains 'a@x.com', and original_message_id or similar references '12345'
criteria.threshold: Valid JSON with required fields emitted for both reply and forward

criteria.description: Reply/forward with a nonexistent message ID produces a clear error mentioning the message was not found
testSteps: Configure mock_osascript to simulate a 'message not found' error when fetching message 99999
Run 'mailctl reply 99999 --body B'
Verify exit code is non-zero
Verify error message on stderr mentions the message or 'not found'
Run 'mailctl forward 99999 --to a@x.com --body B' and verify the same error pattern
criteria.threshold: Nonexistent message ID produces non-zero exit and actionable error on stderr

criteria.description: Reply/forward when Mail.app is not running produces the same error pattern as compose and other commands
testSteps: Configure mock_osascript to simulate Mail.app not running
Run 'mailctl reply 12345 --body B'
Verify exit code is non-zero and stderr mentions Mail.app
Verify the error follows the same pattern as compose (Sprint 5) and messages (Sprint 3)
criteria.threshold: Mail.app-not-running error is consistent across reply/forward and prior commands

criteria.description: 'mailctl --help' lists 'reply' and 'forward' as top-level commands alongside 'compose', 'accounts', 'mailboxes', and 'messages'
testSteps: Run 'mailctl --help' via CliRunner
Verify the output lists 'reply' as a command
Verify the output lists 'forward' as a command
Verify 'compose', 'accounts', 'mailboxes', and 'messages' are still listed (regression)
criteria.threshold: 'reply' and 'forward' visible at the top level alongside all prior commands

criteria.description: 'mailctl reply --help' and 'mailctl forward --help' describe all options including --body, --body-file, --attach, --dangerously-send, --yes, --dry-run, --json, and (for reply) --all; help text warns about --dangerously-send
testSteps: Run 'mailctl reply --help' via CliRunner
Verify help mentions: --body, --body-file, --attach, --dangerously-send, --yes, --dry-run, --json, --all
Verify --dangerously-send help includes a warning about sending
Run 'mailctl forward --help' via CliRunner
Verify help mentions: --to, --body, --body-file, --attach, --dangerously-send, --yes, --dry-run, --json
criteria.threshold: Help for both commands enumerates all options with a clear warning on --dangerously-send

criteria.description: Parametrised safety-model unit tests exist for reply and forward, covering at least 6 bypass-attempt scenarios each, all asserting no 'send' verb appears without --dangerously-send
testSteps: Inspect tests/unit/ for reply/forward safety model tests
Verify parametrised tests cover at minimum: bare reply, reply --yes, reply --dry-run, reply --all, reply with --attach, reply with --body-file; and equivalent for forward
For each parametrisation, verify the test asserts NO 'send' verb in mock_osascript.calls
Run the tests and verify they all pass
criteria.threshold: Safety-model tests cover at least 6 reply and 6 forward bypass scenarios, all passing

criteria.description: Full test suite (pytest from project root) passes with zero failures, including all Sprint 1-5 tests and new Sprint 6 tests
testSteps: Run 'pytest -v' from the project root
Verify all tests pass (0 failures, 0 errors)
Verify Sprint 1-5 tests still pass (regression)
criteria.threshold: All tests pass, exit code 0

criteria.description: Reply and forward implementations follow the established architecture: AppleScript generation in build_*_script() functions, thin Typer command handlers that delegate to perform/fetch functions, and use the shared output/error-handling utilities from prior sprints
testSteps: Inspect the reply/forward implementation files
Verify build_reply_script() and build_forward_script() (or similarly named) functions construct the AppleScript
Verify the command handlers delegate to a perform_reply() / perform_forward() function rather than inlining engine calls
Verify shared utilities (resolve_body, _escape_applescript_string, handle_mail_error, render_error) are reused from compose or output modules, not duplicated
Verify the safety model implementation (--dangerously-send, confirmation, --dry-run) follows the same pattern as compose
criteria.threshold: Reply and forward follow the established build/perform/render pattern with code reuse from compose

criteria.description: All Sprint 1-5 commands still work: 'mailctl --version', accounts list, mailboxes list, messages list/show/search, and compose — with unchanged options and outputs
testSteps: Run 'mailctl --version' and verify version output
Run 'mailctl --help' and verify all command groups are listed including the new reply/forward
Run the full Sprint 1-5 unit test suite and verify all tests pass
criteria.threshold: All Sprint 1-5 functionality preserved; no test regressions

outOfScope: Draft editing — modifying an existing reply/forward draft after creation (Sprint 8)
HTML body composition — reply/forward body is plain text in v1
Inline forwarding of attachments from the original message — only new --attach attachments are supported in v1
Signature insertion — Mail.app may add signatures automatically; mailctl does not manage them
Verifying real email delivery — explicitly forbidden by CLAUDE.md safety guardrails. The send code path is verified via mocked osascript ONLY.

safetyNotes.criticalConstraint: Per /Users/claude/dev/cli.mail.app/CLAUDE.md, no test in this contract may cause a real email to be delivered. Every criterion that exercises the send code path uses mocked osascript subprocess inspection (C-140, C-141) or --dry-run assertion (C-145, C-146). The evaluator MUST NOT run 'mailctl reply --dangerously-send' or 'mailctl forward --dangerously-send' against real Mail.app to verify delivery — that pattern is explicitly forbidden by the project guardrails.
safetyNotes.safeReadOnlyDomain: The evaluator MAY freely run read-only commands against real Mail.app: list accounts, list mailboxes, list/show/search messages. These are non-destructive and were established as safe in prior sprints.
safetyNotes.safeWriteDomain: The evaluator MAY run 'mailctl reply <id> --body Test' or 'mailctl forward <id> --to <own-address> --body Test' (NO --dangerously-send) against real Mail.app to verify a draft appears in the Drafts folder, and then delete that draft. This is permitted per CLAUDE.md's safe-by-default testing domain. All send-path testing must be done via mocks.
